__comment: Sample sources configuration. Copy to sources.json and edit to match the companies you want to track. The 'workday' array supports the Workday CXS public job listings API. Adding a company = adding one entry here.

workday.note: Enable when ready to expand to AI/ML roles

__schema_help.company: URL slug for the tenant. Find in the careers URL: {company}.wd{num}.myworkdayjobs.com
__schema_help.display_name: Human-readable name shown in the dashboard.
__schema_help.site: Site path from the URL. E.g., 'Cisco_Careers' from .../en-US/Cisco_Careers
__schema_help.num: Workday tenant number from the URL (1-99). E.g., 'wd5' -> 5.
__schema_help.enabled: Toggle scraping for this tenant. Disabled tenants are skipped.
__schema_help.tags: Free-form labels for grouping. Used in future analytics.

__how_to_find_a_tenant: 1. Go to the company's careers page (e.g., careers.example.com).
2. Click 'Search Jobs' or any job listing.
3. If the browser URL becomes {something}.wd{N}.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/{Site} -> it's a Workday tenant.
4. Parse the URL: company={something}, num={N}, site={Site}.
5. Add the entry to your sources.json.
